The dispute in this case was about the conviction of Sukhlal @ Prabir Murmu and others in multiple cases of murder and other crimes, with the State of Jharkhand appealing against the acquittal of the accused. The Jharkhand High Court ultimately upheld the acquittal, rejecting the State's appeal. Here is a 2-3 sentence summary of the Indian legal case:

The dispute was about the imposition of the death penalty by a Sessions Judge on three accused individuals, Haribhau Rajaram Telgote, Mrs. Dwarkabai Haribhau Telgote, and Shyam @ Kundan Haribhau Telgote, who were convicted of a crime. The State of Maharashtra, through the Police Station Officer, appealed to the Bombay High Court to confirm the death penalty. The Bombay High Court, in a judgment pronounced on November 13, 2024, upheld the death penalty imposed by the Sessions Judge.
